analysis.summary: With-skill achieves 100% pass rate (18/18) vs baseline 55.6% (10/18). The skill provides the biggest lift on MCP integration (+66.7%) and multi-agent delegation (+50%), where the baseline uses deprecated APIs extensively.
key_findings: Baseline consistently uses deprecated API names: result_type (not output_type), Usage (not RunUsage), result.data (not result.output), MCPServerHTTP (not MCPServerStreamableHTTP), mcp_servers (not toolsets), run_mcp_servers (not async with agent:)
With-skill code correctly uses all v1.x API names and patterns
The MCP eval shows the starkest difference — baseline gets 4 of 6 assertions wrong with deprecated patterns
Multi-agent baseline doesn't use tool-based delegation (uses programmatic pipeline instead), missing the idiomatic pydantic-ai pattern
With-skill agent-tools-output used tool_plain (no RunContext) which is a valid and simpler choice for a tool that doesn't need deps
non_discriminating_assertions: confidence_score constrained to 0-1 (both got this right)
Two agents at module level (both got this right)
Report model with correct fields (both got this right)
